actingAs.stopError: Could not stop acting as. Please try again.

dryness.keep-moist: Keep the soil evenly moist — never let it dry out.
dryness.top-inch-dry: Let the top inch (2–3 cm) of soil dry before watering.
dryness.half-dry: Let the top half of the soil dry before watering.
dryness.mostly-dry: Let the soil dry out almost completely before watering.
dryness.fully-dry: Let the soil dry out completely before watering.

WATER.what: Giving the plant water at the roots.
WATER.why: Too little dries the plant out; too much suffocates the roots and invites rot.
WATER.how: Water until it drains from the bottom, then let the soil dry to this plant's preferred level before the next watering. Empty any saucer so the roots never sit in water.

FERTILIZE.what: Feeding the plant nutrients, usually a diluted liquid fertilizer.
FERTILIZE.why: Potting mix runs out of nutrients; feeding during the growing season keeps growth healthy.
FERTILIZE.how: Apply a balanced fertilizer diluted to the label rate onto already-moist soil, during the active season. Skip a stressed or dormant plant.

REPOT.what: Moving the plant into fresh mix, often a slightly larger pot.
REPOT.why: Roots outgrow the pot and the old mix breaks down, starving and choking the plant.
REPOT.how: Lift the plant out, gently loosen the roots, set it in fresh mix one size up, and water it in. Best in the growing season.

ROTATE.what: Turning the pot so a different side faces the light.
ROTATE.why: Plants lean toward light; rotating keeps growth even and upright.
ROTATE.how: Give the pot a quarter turn each time, always in the same direction, so every side gets its share of light.

CLEAN_LEAVES.why: Dust blocks light and clogs the pores the leaf breathes through, slowing growth.
CLEAN_LEAVES.how: Wipe each leaf gently with a soft, damp cloth, supporting it with your other hand. Do both sides on large leaves.

MIST.what: Spraying a fine mist of water around the foliage.
MIST.why: Briefly raises humidity for plants that come from humid habitats.
MIST.how: Mist the air around the leaves in the morning so they dry by night; avoid soaking fuzzy-leaved plants, which trap water and spot.

PROGRESS.what: Logging a photo and a note about how the plant is doing.
PROGRESS.why: A record of size and health over time shows whether your care is working.
PROGRESS.how: Snap a photo, jot an observation, and optionally record the height so the care plan can track growth.

repot.crowdingBody: This date is an inspection reminder, not a verdict. It moves earlier when your plant grows tall for its pot (a crowded plant is more likely to be root-bound) and later when it sits in a roomy one. The roots decide — go and look.
repot.signsEmpty: Check whether roots are circling the pot or growing out of the drainage holes.

careBasis.title: The care plan is based on

generic.title: How to measure
generic.body: Measure the plant's longest dimension — usually its height from the soil surface to the tallest growing point. For a plant that trails or hangs, measure the longest stem from the pot rim to its tip.

upright.title: Measuring an upright plant
upright.body: Measure the height from the soil surface straight up to the tallest leaf tip or growing point. Hold the tape vertical — don't follow a leaning stem.

climber.title: Measuring a climber
climber.body: Measure along the main vine from the soil to its growing tip, following the stem even where it's tied to a pole or trellis.

trailing.title: Measuring a trailing plant
trailing.body: Measure the single longest stem from the pot rim to its tip. Let the vine hang or lay it straight — don't add the stems together.

clumping.title: Measuring a clumping plant
clumping.body: Measure the tallest leaf or stem from the soil to its tip. The clump spreads sideways, so height is the number that changes as it grows.

rosette.title: Measuring a rosette
rosette.body: Measure the diameter across the widest part of the rosette, from leaf tip to opposite leaf tip over the top of the plant.

tree.title: Measuring a tree form
tree.body: Measure the height from the soil surface to the top of the canopy. Ignore the pot — measure the plant itself.

shrub.title: Measuring a shrub
shrub.body: Measure the height from the soil to the tallest branch tip. If it's wider than it is tall, note the width in your observations.

other.title: How to measure
other.body: Measure the plant's longest dimension — usually height from the soil to the tallest growing point; for a trailing plant, the longest stem from the pot rim to the tip.

plantProfile.title: Plant details
plantProfile.subtitle: Optional facts about this specific plant. Fill in what you know.
plantProfile.potSizeHint: Measure rim-to-rim across the top — not the radius or the height.
